component.name: DLSS Updater
component.summary: Update DLSS, FSR, and XeSS DLLs for your games

description.p: DLSS Updater automatically detects and updates NVIDIA DLSS, AMD FSR, and Intel XeSS DLLs across your entire game library. It supports multiple game launchers including Steam, Epic Games, GOG Galaxy, Ubisoft Connect, EA Play, Battle.net, and Xbox Game Pass.
Features:

ul.li: Cross-platform support for Windows and Linux
Automatic detection of games from multiple launchers
Support for Steam Proton and Wine prefixes on Linux
Built-in backup system for restoring original DLLs
Updates for DLSS, FSR, XeSS, Ray Reconstruction, Frame Generation, and Streamline
Individual game updates for specific binaries
Custom folder support for games in non-standard locations

screenshot.caption: Main application window showing detected games

description.p: New Proton Upscalers panel for Linux

ul.li: The "Linux DLSS SR Presets" panel is now a per-title Proton upscaler configurator covering all three GPU vendors
DLSS Ray Reconstruction preset override and Frame Generation override via DXVK-NVAPI (parity with the Windows per-game dialog), plus new "Latest" and "Preset J" SR preset choices
DLSS / FSR 4 / XeSS DLL upgrade toggles and on-screen indicators via the community Proton forks' launch options (GE-Proton, Proton-CachyOS, Proton-EM), with an RDNA3/RDNA4 mode switch for FSR 4
Per-game validation: the panel reads Steam's compat-tool mapping to show which Proton build a selected game runs under and disables upgrade toggles that build doesn't ship
GPU vendor auto-detection (NVIDIA/AMD/Intel) gates the vendor sections and recommends RDNA3 mode on RX 7000 GPUs
Backwards compatible: configs saved by older versions generate identical launch options, and the generated string still works as environment variables in Heroic/Lutris

description.p: Modernized async/dependency stack, plus fixes found through it

ul.li: Replaced the DLL-download fallback library (requests, now in upstream feature-freeze) with niquests, a maintained drop-in replacement with HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 support
Settings storage migrated from config.ini to config.toml (via msgspec) with an automatic, non-destructive one-time migration - the old config.ini is left untouched
Adopted anyio for structured concurrency across the update/scan/database pipeline, consolidating several separate thread pools into shared, properly-bounded worker limiters
Logging now runs through a background queue instead of blocking on every log call
Fixed a deadlock in the high-performance batch DLL updater that could freeze the update progress dialog partway through a large batch
Fixed the Games Library not reflecting updated DLL versions after a batch update if the Games tab hadn't been opened yet that session
Fixed HyperParallelLoader briefly blocking the UI event loop while dispatching thread-pool work

description.p: Linux update reliability fix (Issue #240)

ul.li: Fixed the source-DLL memory cache using a Windows-only os.O_BINARY flag, which made caching fail for every DLL on Linux and forced a slower file-copy fallback on every scan

Fixed Steam games being scanned and updated twice when Steam's default install path is a symlink (e.g. Linux's ~/.steam/steam -> ~/.local/share/Steam), which could also cause a spurious update failure for one of the duplicate attempts
is_file_in_use() no longer surfaces a raw error if the target file disappears between the existence check and the check itself

description.p: Per-game DLSS preset overrides (Windows) and a Games Library redesign

ul.li: New per-game "DLSS Settings" panel forces SR/RR/FG presets for a single game via its NVIDIA per-application driver profile; it takes priority over the global override and applies at the next launch (Windows + NVIDIA only)
Automatic game-executable detection with a "Change executable" file-picker fallback; the executable and preset choices are remembered, and "Reset to default" reverts in one click

Redesigned game cards with a full-bleed hero banner using Steam's higher-quality "library_hero" artwork (replacing the old small capsule image), reframed to avoid the plain space some banners have near the top
The DLL status badge and Update/Restore buttons are now compact icons that expand on hover, with a fixed-height footer so they can no longer be clipped or hidden when resizing the window
Per-game actions are consolidated into a single "⋮" menu on each card (the Play button was removed and Launch moved into the menu; the Hide and Edit icons remain on the card)
Fixed SQLite foreign-key enforcement so removing a game correctly cleans up its related backup, ignore-list, DLL, and update-history rows

description.p: Linux and Flatpak fixes for game detection (Issue #228)

ul.li: Folder paths now expand "~" and environment variables (e.g. ~/.steam)
Adding a folder outside the Flatpak sandbox now shows the filesystem permission instructions instead of a misleading "does not exist" error
Auto-Detect now finds Steam libraries on Linux (native and Flatpak installs) instead of relying on the Windows-only registry

description.p: Desktop UX and UI improvements across the game library, launchers, and settings

ul.li: Light/dark theme switching is now live with a subtle cascade animation — no application restart required
Game cards now have a right-click context menu (Update all DLLs, Restore from backup, Open folder, Copy path, Launch via Steam, Ignore/Unignore, Edit display) that reflects each card's current state

Games Library search is now a native search field with live game-name suggestions and recent-search history, plus status filter chips (Needs update / Up to date / Has backups); it collapses to a search icon and expands on click
Settings view redesigned with a denser two-column responsive tile layout and an inline light/dark theme toggle on the Theme tile
Launchers tab uses a two-column responsive card layout and shows a "rescan recommended" prompt when the last scan is stale (7+ days) or missing
Home page Games card gained a stats strip showing DLL backup count and size plus time since the last scan
Undo support: removing a launcher path or toggling a game's ignored state now offers an "Undo" action in the snackbar, which auto-dismisses and is legible in both themes
Floating action pill moved to the bottom-left and no longer overlaps the launcher action bar or scrollable content
Slim, rounded, auto-hiding scrollbars across all scroll areas; log count badge caps at "99+"; window minimum size increased to 820x560 to prevent clipping at narrow widths

description.p: Edit game, plus UI polish and header declutter

ul.li: New "Edit game" button on every game card lets you manually update or change a game's name or image via one that exists on Steam. (Issue #202)
Search uses the local FTS5 app-list database (no credentials required); Steam API credentials improve result quality when configured
The override is cosmetic only — backups, merge keys, and the global updater are unaffected; the original folder-derived name is preserved internally
Override survives rescans: stored in dedicated columns the scanner never touches, so a rescan cannot revert a manually set link
Existing overrides can be cleared from the same dialog with one click
Fixed hub card stat badges ("3 configured", "14 games found") showing an incorrect yellowish tint; they now display a proper subtle accent-tinted background in both light and dark modes
Games Library header decluttered: "Hide ignored games" and "Delete Database" moved into a compact options menu, keeping only search, options, and refresh visible
Search bar now collapses to an icon by default and expands to full width on click; auto-collapses when cleared and focus is lost
Removed the "Application" top-bar button; "Check for Updates" is now a tile in the Settings view

description.p: New DLSS Settings: global DLSS preset overrides for SR / RR / FG (Windows)

ul.li: A new "DLSS Settings" card on the home page lets you force global DLSS presets for every game via the NVIDIA driver's NvAPI Driver Settings — the same base-profile mechanism the NVIDIA App uses. Super Resolution exposes Default/Latest/Preset J/K/L/M; Ray Reconstruction and Frame Generation expose Default/Latest model. Applied the next time each game launches; the panel shows the currently applied driver preset for each component on open

Note: the NVIDIA App's DLSS override also writes per-game overrides that take priority over this global setting for those games. Set the DLSS override to Default/Off in the NVIDIA App to let this global setting apply (the panel explains this inline)
Windows + NVIDIA only; the existing Linux DLSS SR preset panel (DXVK-NVAPI launch options) is unchanged
Dependency upgrade: flet and flet-desktop 0.85.0 -> 0.85.2 (Windows); restored nvidia-ml-py for GPU detection

description.p: Fixed Linux Flatpak failing to launch on Steam Deck / CachyOS / strict W^X kernels (Issue #217)

ul.li: v4.1.5 Flatpak failed to start with "cannot enable executable stack as shared object requires: Invalid argument" because the upstream python-build-standalone 3.14.3+freethreaded artifact (introduced in v4.1.5) ships libpython3.14t.so.1.0 with PT_GNU_STACK marked executable (RWE). Modern Linux kernels enforce W^X and refuse dlopen() on shared objects requesting an executable stack, returning EINVAL

v4.1.4 used the 3.14.2+freethreaded artifact which had a clean GNU_STACK (RW); the regression is in the upstream 3.14.3 build, not the DLSS Updater code. Verified end-to-end with readelf -lW on both artifacts (RW on 3.14.2, RWE on 3.14.3)

build_flatpak.sh now runs `execstack -c` on the uv-managed Python install immediately after `uv python install`, clearing the executable-stack flag from every .so file before PyInstaller bundles them. A readelf-based hard-verify step refuses to proceed if libpython still has GNU_STACK = RWE, so a broken artifact can never silently ship again
execstack added to the apt and dnf install dependencies in build_flatpak.sh. (On Arch, execstack lives in AUR — install manually with `yay -S execstack` if building on Arch)
Build-pipeline-only fix — no application code changes. The Windows MSI is unaffected (Windows free-threaded Python ships without an executable-stack program header)

description.p: Fixed Windows 11 HDR being disabled when opening the folder picker (Issue #216)

ul.li: On Windows 11 (notably 24H2 with HDR enabled), clicking "Add Sub-Folder" on a launcher card disabled system HDR while the file dialog was open

Root cause: the FilePicker was created but never registered on page.overlay, so the underlying Win32 IFileOpenDialog opened as an unowned top-level window (NULL hwndOwner) that crossed the Flet window's focus group, tripping the per-app HDR auto-arbitration

FilePicker is now instantiated inline at the point of use, matching the Flet 0.85 Service pattern — the Service base class auto-attaches to the active page so the dialog inherits the Flet window's focus group and HDR is no longer disabled
Upgraded flet 0.84.0 to 0.85.0 and flet-desktop 0.84.0 to 0.85.0 (declarative router, use_dialog hook, video/audio improvements; no FilePicker or window/HDR changes)
Migrated 137 call sites across 30 files from the removed ft.padding/margin/border/border_radius module helpers to the new ft.Padding/Margin/Border/BorderRadius classmethods (hard removal in 0.85)
Upgraded requests 2.33.1 to 2.34.0 and idna 3.13 to 3.14 (transitive)

Pinned Python to 3.14.3+freethreaded (was 3.14+freethreaded) for both Windows and Linux builds — highest 3.14 free-threaded build currently published by astral-sh/python-build-standalone on both platforms (Linux lags Windows on patch releases). PyInstaller specs continue to pass -X gil=0 at the bootloader level. All CI/CD workflows (python-app, pylint, build-flatpak, update-download-badge, codspeed) bumped to match — codspeed in particular was previously benchmarking on regular GIL-enabled CPython 3.14 rather than the free-threaded interpreter the app actually ships with

Audited all 34 cp314t native extensions: only msgpack 1.1.2 still triggers the GIL re-enable warning (Cython freethreading_compatible directive landed upstream post-1.1.2 and will ship in the next release). PYTHON_GIL=0 is now also set in the Flatpak finish-args as defense-in-depth so the GIL stays disabled at runtime on Linux

Migrated license metadata to PEP 639 format (SPDX `license = "AGPL-3.0-only"` plus `license-files = ["LICENSE"]` in both [project] and [tool.briefcase]) to resolve the deprecation warning emitted by Briefcase 0.4.2 during MSI packaging

description.p: Security patch and dependency upgrades (Issue #210)

ul.li: Bumped GitPython 3.1.49 to 3.1.50 to patch a newline-injection bypass of CVE-2026-42215 in config_writer().set_value() (the section/option arguments were not validated, allowing a forged [core] hooksPath header and RCE via git hooks)
GitPython is only used at MSI build time (transitive via briefcase), so shipped binaries were not exploitable, but the vulnerable resolution has been removed from the lock file

Upgraded briefcase 0.4.1 to 0.4.2, rich 14.3.4 to 15.0.0, urllib3 2.6.3 to 2.7.0, tomlkit 0.14.0 to 0.15.0, propcache 0.4.1 to 0.5.2, markdown-it-py 4.0.0 to 4.2.0, pyinstaller-hooks-contrib 2026.4 to 2026.5, pip 26.1 to 26.1.1
No runtime or free-threaded Python 3.14t behaviour changes

description.p: Update summary now shows actual game names (Issue #207)

ul.li: Fixed: the Update Complete dialog showed "common" for every game instead of the actual game name
Game names are now resolved using launcher-aware path parsing (Steam, EA, Epic, GOG, Ubisoft, custom folders)
Standard update fallback path also fixed: results were passed as raw tuples instead of formatted strings

description.p: Windows taskbar pinning fix (Issue #184)

ul.li: Fixed: pinning the running app to the taskbar previously pinned flet.exe (the Flet GUI engine), causing a white screen when relaunching from the pin
AppUserModelID and RelaunchCommand are now set on flet.exe's window at startup so Windows pins DLSS_Updater.exe instead
Start Menu shortcut in the MSI installer now carries the System.AppUserModel.ID property for correct taskbar association

description.p: DLL rollback compatibility warning and Flatpak runtime upgrade (Issue #204)

ul.li: New DLL Rollback Compatibility Warning: before updating, the app cross-references the target DLL version against your own restore history and warns you if you've rolled back from that exact version in two or more other games within the past 48 hours
Warning dialog offers three choices: Cancel, Skip flagged DLLs, or Update anyway — the decision is yours
Inline amber badge in the DLL Details dialog flags any DLL whose latest version matches a version you've recently rolled back from
All detection is local SQL against your own backup history — no telemetry, nothing leaves your machine
Upgraded Flatpak runtime from Freedesktop 24.08 to 25.08 to fix install failures on newer Fedora versions
25.08 ships libsecret natively and brings glibc 2.42 and GCC 15.1
Fixed: Update button stayed enabled on every game regardless of DLL version — it now disables when all DLLs are current, and the dropdown only lists groups with an outdated DLL
Fixed: Restore menu now refreshes immediately after an update so newly-created backups appear without re-opening the view; bulk updates re-sync Restore menus via a single batched query
Fixed: rollback compatibility detection was keying on the wrong version (the version rolled back TO, not FROM). Warnings now fire on the correct version.

Changed the flag threshold from "two or more distinct games" to "two or more total rollback events" for the same (DLL, version). Vendor-signed DLLs are identical across every game, so each rollback is an independent vote against that version, even when repeated in the same game.

description.p: Personal game ignore list, DLL status indicators, and UX improvements

ul.li: Personal game ignore list: exclude specific games from updates via image overlay toggle (Issue #194)
Ignored Games settings panel for bulk management with search
Ignore filter moved to header bar; action buttons no longer misalign at narrow widths
Game cards show color-coded outdated/current DLL badges
DLL badges auto-refresh after Update All and Refresh Games (reads versions from disk)
Backup disk usage summary in Games view header
Enhanced empty-state UX for launcher path cards
Game launch button for Steam games via steam:// protocol

description.p: Clipboard fix and Flet upgrade

ul.li: Fixed clipboard copy button error in Linux DLSS panel (Issue #189)
Upgraded Flet from 0.83.0 to 0.84.0 on Linux

description.p: Launcher UX improvements, Steam multi-library detection, and window state persistence

ul.li: Window State Persistence: App remembers window size, position, and maximized state between sessions (Issue #183)
Steam Image Fix: Fallback to Steam appdetails API for newer games using hash-based CDN URLs (Issue #182)
Rescan Prompt: Snackbar with "Rescan" action button appears after removing or resetting launcher paths (Issue #181)
Steam Multi-Library Auto-Detect: Auto-Detect now discovers all Steam library folders across multiple drives via libraryfolders.vdf (Issue #180)
Launcher Page Refresh Fix: LauncherCard now correctly uses self.update() for isolated control updates (Issue #179)

description.p: UI Fix - DLL Details dialog now scales responsively with window size
Bug Fixes - Scanner crash and image loading

ul.li: Fixed UnboundLocalError in find_all_dlls() where a redundant local import of config_manager shadowed the module-level import
Fixed "can not serialize WindowsPath object" error when loading uncached game images
Scan now correctly reads user DLL update preferences and loads all game images without errors

description.p: MSI Build Fix - Free-threaded Python runtime (Issue #176)

ul.li: Fixed MSI installer failing to start with "Disabling the GIL is not supported by this build"
MSI GitHub Actions workflow now uses Python 3.14t (free-threaded) instead of standard 3.14
Ensures the bundled Python interpreter supports the -X gil=0 flag required by the application

description.p: Steam API Integration - Dramatically improved game image loading (Issue #175)

ul.li: Added optional Steam API key support for accurate game app ID resolution
Four-tier resolution: Manifest → Steam API → Store Search → FTS5 fuzzy match
Steam API card on Games tab with API key input, validation, and auto-detected Steam ID
One-click re-resolution to fix images from previous scans when API key is added
Smart cache invalidation: only re-fetches images when app ID actually changes
Resolution source tracking for intelligent re-resolution decisions

description.p: MSI installer for Windows, resolving Windows Defender false positive issues

ul.li: Added MSI installer build using PyInstaller (onedir) + Briefcase for winget and Chocolatey
Resolves Windows Defender false positive (Trojan:Win32/Wacatac.H!ml) blocking winget submission
MSI installs to Program Files with proper Add/Remove Programs entry
Portable single-exe distribution remains available via direct download
Updated winget manifest to InstallerType: msi
Updated Chocolatey package to use MSI with proper msiexec install/uninstall

description.p: Flet 0.83.0 upgrade, bug fixes, and dependency updates

ul.li: Upgraded Flet from 0.82.2 to 0.83.0 with internal performance engine improvements (up to 6.7x for animated UIs)
Removed flet-desktop-light (deprecated); Linux now uses flet-desktop with automatic light flavor
Fixed navigation crash when switching away from Settings and back
Fixed logger panel toggle button not expanding/collapsing
Fixed incorrect "Latest" DLL version numbers in the Games DLL Details dialog (Issue #167)
Updated winloop 0.6.0, packaging 26.0, aiohttp 3.13.4, aiosqlite 0.22.1, platformdirs 4.9.4

description.p: Bug fixes

ul.li: Fix for progressive loading of games.
Adjustments to the default Linux flatpak permissions to read/write to allow writing by default.
Some upgrades to dependencies to fix CVE reports.

description.p: Data Lifecycle and UX Improvements

ul.li: Phantom Game Cleanup: Uninstalled games are now automatically removed from the database on rescan
Version Format Normalization: DLL versions consistently display in dot-separated format
DLL Count Consistency: Fixed issue where updating custom folder games could reduce DLL count
MergedGame AttributeError: Fixed crash when using Update/Restore on duplicate game entries
Stale Version Display: DLL versions refreshed from filesystem before showing dialog
Database Cleanup: Automatic cleanup of duplicate games, backups, and orphan DLL records
Shutdown Progress Dialog: Visual feedback during app shutdown showing cleanup progress

description.p: Navigation Transition Performance Optimization

ul.li: Reduced navigation transitions from ~600ms to under 100ms (most views)
Content detachment: only the active view is serialized during page updates
Single page.update() per transition instead of 2 (no fade-out gap)
GamesView isolation: 500+ control subtree excluded from navigation updates
Hub/Settings transitions ~10x faster, Games transitions ~10-18x faster

description.p: Hub-Based Navigation Redesign

ul.li: Replaced tab-based navigation with a hub home screen
Added floating pill navigation bar shown when inside views
Navigation stack supports back navigation via Escape key
New Settings view for Update Preferences, UI Preferences, Blacklist, Theme
Removed standalone Backups tab (available per-game in Games view)

description.p: Flet 0.80.4 Migration - Major framework upgrade

ul.li: Upgraded Flet from 0.28.3 to 0.80.4 (major version jump)
Modernized entry point and API usage
Updated all UI components for new Flet patterns
Improved GIL unlock support for free-threaded Python 3.14

description.p: GUI Unresponsiveness Fix - Critical fix for frozen/unclickable UI

ul.li: Fixed LoadingOverlay blocking all input after hiding (caused frozen UI)
Removed darkdetect dependency that caused blocking startup on Linux/Wayland
Fixed admin elevation race condition causing dual process instances on Windows
Theme now loads from saved preference or defaults to dark mode

description.p: Bug Fixes and Improvements

ul.li: Enables the use of the light theme, found under Preferences
Bug fixes for memory leak issues
DLL's are now categorised under Games, for better legibility.

description.p: Linux Flatpak Fixes - Resolved multiple launch and scan issues

ul.li: Fixed FileNotFoundError when ~/.flet/bin directory doesn't exist (Issue #127)
Fixed OSError "Read-only file system" on Flatpak by ensuring Flet directories are created (Issue #122)
Added --filesystem=~/.flet:create permission to Flatpak build script
Fixed "Scan Failed" error caused by dict being passed as path (Issue #126)
Fixed GTK/SVG icon loading failures on Nobara 43 (libstdc++ CXXABI conflict)
Excluded system C++ libraries from PyInstaller bundle to use Flatpak runtime libraries

description.p: Intel XeSS Frame Generation Support

ul.li: Added libxess_fg.dll (XeSS Frame Generation SDK v1.2.2.118)
Added libxell.dll (Intel XeLL library v1.2.1.13)
Updated libxess.dll and libxess_dx11.dll to v2.0.2.68
Intel XeSS Frame Generation provides AI-powered frame interpolation for Arc GPUs
Fixes for multiple drives having the same game, and fixes for sub-root detection of DLL's not merging correctly

description.p: Flatpak distribution replaces AppImage for improved Linux compatibility

ul.li: Flatpak package available on Flathub and GitHub Releases
Eliminates library compatibility issues across different Linux distributions
Consistent runtime environment with all dependencies included
In-app dialog guides users to grant permissions for custom game locations
AppImage distribution discontinued in favor of Flatpak
Removed sudo/root elevation support (Flatpak handles permissions)

description.p: DLSS SR Preset Override - GPU-based preset recommendations

ul.li: Configure DLSS Super Resolution presets (K/L/M) with automatic GPU detection
RTX 20/30 series recommended Preset K, RTX 40/50 series use Preset M or K
Preset L is heavier and may reduce performance
Windows: System-wide registry override for all games
Linux: Generate Steam launch options with copy-to-clipboard
First-time setup dialog appears automatically for NVIDIA GPU users
Re-configurable anytime via Preferences menu

description.p: Linux improvements - removed sudo requirement

ul.li: Removed forced sudo/root requirement for Linux
App now runs in user space by default
System-level Wine/Proton paths skipped with warning banner
Most Steam Proton/Lutris games work without elevation

description.p: Added Linux support

ul.li: Cross-platform support for Windows and Linux
Steam Proton and Wine prefix detection on Linux
Bundled dependencies for standalone distribution

description.p: Performance improvements and bug fixes
